entry.title: Re: Automatic Freight Unit Planning Multimodal
entry.content: 👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻 very good Rogerio

entry.title: Re: Automatic set of “defaultLocale” standard-element based on employee’s country in employee centra
entry.content:
Hi, as well would like to hear on the case raised by @IWS. @sandbisw or other experts @lukemarson  can you relplay what will happen for already existing emploees when currently default locale is EN_US and then based on the business rule it will be EN_GB. Will that be automaic change on their settings or that will require data reload for existing employees? And only for the new hires rule will work. 

Thank you 

Kasia

entry.title: Re: How to use SAP GUI Scripting inside Python Programming Language
entry.content: @former_member729110 You are right that Scripting Tracker doesn't show GuiTree nodes or GuiGridView cells. The good news is you don't have to implement the logic for traversing them. It is already built into RoboSAPiens (https://pypi.org/project/robotframework-robosapiens/). This library offers a uniform API for all tabular components in the SAP GUI. You can address any cell using as coordinates the column title and either the row number or some text in the row.

entry.title: Re: Initial Release of the Lite Viewer for SAP Analytics Cloud Stories
entry.content:
More precision from my side :

and prtscrns

if you share a story :

and put in the end of the URL : ?shellMode=embed this has no effect :

 

but if you put at the end of URL ?mode=embed everything is ok :

 

 

Exactly the same with customized links :

this one has no effect :

 

But this one is ok :

 

You have to differenciate a link that you copy after loading the story in the Adressbar and a link that you use for sharing your stories.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: TM master data based Sales Order Scheduling
entry.content: Hi Michael -  Many thanks for insights! Noted that we must upgrade to SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 for this solution. Best-Ajit

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 01
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Master Data Governance can be the backbone for enterprises that ensures high master data quality:

  • when they are planning to go for digital transformation or other structural optimization processes (such as M&A, divestitures, corporate restructuring)
  • as a solid data layer when implementing AI-based processes
  • for streamlined business transactions across lines of business and reliable company analytics

Latest Innovations

Recently, I outlined the innovations and associated benefits of using SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023, and provided additional details about the cloud-ready mode and federated governance.

I’d like to continue with information about innovations in master data governance that come with the Feature Pack Stack 01 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 and SAP S/4HANA 2023 (on premise). New features come both in central governance across domains (classic mode), in mass processing and consolidation (classic mode), in business partner processing (cloud-ready mode), and in data quality management (for classic mode and cloud-ready mode).

Cross-domain innovations for central governance in classic mode

Central governance of business partner (incl. customer and supplier data)

  • Automatically refreshed change requests after master data updates to ensure that backend changes on master data objects are not overwritten during change request activation so that intended backend changes as well as changes using a change request are compatible and consistent.
  • Validation of data in change request to include unchanged data.

Fig.: Business validations are always performed for changed segments and for the following segments: address postal and related communication data, central data of the business partner and customer/ supplier, bank accounts, industries, and tax numbers.

Central governance of material data

  • Automatically refreshed change requests after master data updates.

Central governance of financial data

  • Support more attributes for account and cost center in initial load.
  • Prevent duplicate cost element groups in chart of accounts to make sure that no identical IDs are in use across controlling areas that use the same chart of account, which is relevant for flawless replication.

Innovations for consolidation and mass processing in classic mode

  • Assignment of data controllers via file upload, which can be used to control access to business partner data.​
  • Configurable address handling in best record calculation​.
  • Data model extension (product)​.

Fig.: Best record calculation: merging all data controllers to best record

Innovations for business partner processing in cloud-ready mode

  • Federation: Facilitate that ​SAP S/4HANA systems running the classic mode in MDG can be integrated in a federated governance scenario as application data owners (see also the how-to guide). 
  • Assignment of data controllers​.
  • Configurable address handling in best record calculation​.

Innovations for data quality management

  • Support derivation of class assignments and characteristics for product, customer, and supplier data.
  • Include additional result tables in derivation scenarios, that is:
    • for business partners: Financial Services Details (BP001) and Credit Master Data (UKMBP_CMS)
    • for products: Change Management (MARA_AE0I)
  • Option to disable several derivation scenarios at once.
  • In cloud-ready mode only: select process template for data remediation.

Fig.: ​Derivation of class assignments and characteristics is now supported for products, customer and supplier master data.

From the outlined innovations, you can tell that both classic mode in MDG and cloud-ready mode are continuously enhanced. The driver for innovations in classic mode is primarily the SAP Continuous Influence program, whereas the ABAP cloud based cloud-ready mode in MDG is the strategic development path going forward.

To see the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio up to 2025, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

Stay on track and simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: TM master data based Sales Order Scheduling
entry.content:
Hi Michael,

Thanks for this blog. This is one of the most desired functionalities for most of the customers.

It is stated "During TM scheduling, no capacities are considered and no optimizer functionality is used". Does this mean freight order is to be created manually (i.e not through optimizer) ? Many customers use Optimizer. Please inform the roadmap.

With regards,

Mrinal

entry.title: Consumption for Component from DM not executed in S/4 Backend
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

we're struggling with the consumption posting triggered from DM to our S/4 Backend (Production Order Scenario).

In DM everything seems to be fine but in S/4 the confirmation remains in the "predefined confirmation process".

The component is waiting for the consumption posting with movement type 261. Before that production supply was done by MIGO with movement type 311 to DM-managed destination storage location.

(nothing is happening when we trigger the postprocess...)

We checked all steps described in the integration guide

1) Control Key which does not allow Auto Goods Receipt:

2) Operation Sequence not checked

3) Define immediate posting triggered by PDC System

Do we miss a step here?

I'd be glad about any suggestions.

Thank you!


BR,

Christoph

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: Improved integration of purchase scheduling agreements with TM
entry.content:
Hi @Michael_Haase ,

thanks for the blog and the great explanation of the new feature.

I was tricked by the following sentence:

"Thereby, a schedule line has open quantity when the order quantity is lower than the goods receipt quantity."

It should be the other way around, right?

And one additional question regarding the safety margin. This is always taken into account from the present date meaning the date of the (re-)calculation of the time range, right? Maybe this could be more highlighted.

Best regards
Malte

entry.title: Shift the paradigm of supply chain planning by reflecting external risks
entry.content:
In a constantly changing and unstable world the need for companies to proactively anticipate risks and manage also external risks actively already in supply chain planning is key to ensure a resilient, reliable, and sustainable operation of their supply chains.

Everstream’s predictive risk insights and analytics tell you what matters, when it matters and the impact anywhere in the supply chain from geopolitical shifts, material shortages and global regulations to the consequences of extreme weather.

The Everstream Analytics integration with SAP IBP enables a customer to benefit from a risk-aware supply chain planning by empowering a supply chain planner or data analyst to actively and timely reflecting external risks affecting your supply chain also in the supply chain planning process.

A company can integrate external incident data from Everstream Analytics into SAP Integrated Business Planning using the SAP Integration Suite. An incident is a concrete risk, e. g., a severe weather event predicted to happen or already happening. At the end th.ere are many risk categories, for example infrastructure shutdown, labor strikes, etc. All may cause supply chain disruptions and are so relevant to be known and reflected timely in supply chain planning.

This will allow supply chain planners and data analysts to perform risk-aware planning using internal and external risks in the following ways:

  • Discover and subsequently mitigate risks throughout their supply chain network to avoid crisis management and boost the resiliency of their business
  • Use real-time information about events and disruptions from the external world to assess their impact on their business
  • Utilize external data for causal analysis to enhance accuracy of their plans

Business Value

  • Reduce revenue at risk
  • Assess also potential opportunities due to external incidents
  • Ensure service levels, including fulfilling critical customer orders
  • Alerting and managing of risks in time and use what-if scenarios for different external risk events in planning
  • Be more agile in responding on supply chain disruptions

 

Capabilities:

Planners will be able to display the risk score data in apps such as Planner Workspaces and Manage Analytics Stories, gain visibility and identify events that can impact supply chain planning positively or negatively.

  • Extended Visibility using Extend KPI dashboard by external risks
  • Leverage risk-based alerting by using risk scores and locations in customized alerts, e.g., risk score > 8 and production receipts > 5000
  • Planning with risks and risk scores to see how locations are affected by risks over time and react accordingly
  • Contextual navigation to Everstream Analytics from a risk in SAP IBP to more details on this risk in the Everstream platform

 

Configuration:

With the integration to Everstream Analytics you can replicate locations with geo coordinates that planners want to monitor for external incidents from SAP IBP to Everstream Analytics.

External incidents are stored in SAP IBP as risk and opportunity master data with a risk score defined as a key figure for the locations and for the validity period of the risk. 

The sample model entity SAPIBP1 has been enhanced to support the integration.

Master Data:

  • Location: New flag has been added to identify the locations that need to be replicated
  • Risk and Opportunities: Extended to integrate the information from Everstream Analytics

Planning Area:

  • Planning level at risk and opportunities and day level
  • External risk score key figure to integrate the risk score into time series

 

Integration:

The integration is realized via the SAP Integration Suite. A new integration package containing integration flows (iFlows) is available to enable the replication from and to Everstream Analytics. This integration package is available on the API Business Accelerator Hub and allows to streamline the integration of the two systems.

Three iFlows are available :

  • Two iFlows to read the SAP IBP Location Master Data and replicate the information over to Everstream Analytics for monitoring and identifying incidents
  • One iFlow to read the incidents from Everstream Analytics and send them with their risk score back to SAP IBP

Integration Flow | SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain Integration with Everstream Analytics | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 

Let’s start to shift the paradigm of supply chain planning by empowering the planner to reflecting external risks via unlocking the potential of SAP IBP and Everstream Analytics!

 

Learn more about SAP IBP and Everstream Analytics

  • Partner Store - Supply Chain Risk Management byResilience360 Inc. dba Everstream
  • Demo Video Import Supply Chain Risk Data from Everstream Analytics
  • SAP Digital Planning success stories
  • SAP IBP product tour
  • www.sap.com/ibp
  • Homepage - Everstream AI

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: Improved integration of purchase scheduling agreements with TM
entry.content:
Hi Malte,
Thanks for your comment!
Of course you're right...it's obviously the other way round and I've just corrected it. Thanks for the hint!
And yes, the safety margin refers to the present date as you explain it.
Best regards,
Michael

entry.title: [SAP B1] License and Authorization Concern
entry.content:
We have professional and limited licenses (CRM, Financials and Logistics). Now, in some situations, we need to temporarily allow a certain user to access (read & write) a module outside the scope of his/her license type. For example, a user with "limited financials license" needs to access modules only accessible when using a "limited CRM license".

My idea is to give the user authorization to the necessary modules and also lend that user a professional license. Will this work without any issues? Will the already set authorizations get reset after I give the user a license type different from the one he/she normally uses? By the time I need to revoke the temporary access for those modules, I only need to change his/her license type back to limited, and those modules will be inaccessible even if I leave the authorization as is. And when the user needs to access those modules again, I only need to lend her a professional license. Is my understanding correct?

entry.title: Re: capture filename from azure storage adapter
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

your solution worked thank you.

I have another question , what if the blob path places different filenames like below

1234_massorder.txt

5678_massorder.txt

how to define my path in content modifier exchange property.

as of now i defined as Mass_Order_Participant_19gfpaqj9fptegx7vbaacpbh/MassOrderUpload/testtxtfile.txt

Input 

Mass_Order_Participant_19gfpaqj9fptegx7vbaacpbh/MassOrderUpload/1234_massorder.txt

Mass_Order_Participant_19gfpaqj9fptegx7vbaacpbh/MassOrderUpload/5678_massorder.txt

Output

1234_massorder.txt

5678_massorder.txt

entry.title: Re: Reached timeout error for SAPUI5 application launched in SAP CRM WebUI
entry.content: Hello gregorw, I have the same issue but on a btp cap application when displayed on an iframe, and I still haven't found a solution that solves it!

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between "tables:X" and "ls_str TYPE X"?
entry.content: The difference is that TABLES is also used for automatic Dynpro field transport, memory sharing (obsolete), logical database (obsolete). See TABLES documentation.

entry.title: Re: Delete Selection Text Issue
entry.content:
This option is disabled already.
Menu Edit --> Modification Operations--> Switch Off Assistant.
When i go to uttlilities->adjust->selection text.. I am getting an message 
Text elements ZGXX_FI_DOCUMNT_BUS_DASHBOARD modified. Text comparison can only be displayed.

entry.title: Re: Error while adding subscription for SAP Build Apps se...

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello @MarinaDB welcome to SAP Community and I am sorry, that you feel lost. Did you do the Tour the Community tutorial already? That tutorial can help you to understand SAP Community better and you can also earn a badge! Furthermore, you can visit the SAP Community FAQ page. 

I think your question would better fit in the Technology Q&A. There might be more experts there to help you. Be sure, you add the correct tag for SAP SuccessFactors. You can find all SAP managed tags here.

I hope you'll get your answer soon!

entry.title: IRM Restriction on FSV - Different behavior with Define FSV in Global Hierarchies
entry.content:
Hello,

I have actually two questions for which I was not able to find any input and related to same feature : Manage FSV in Global Hierarchies

  1. I did not find any information regarding Migrate FSV to Global Hierarchies deprecation in R2402, is this expected and documented? Looking at the documentation, it is still visible but the feature and configuration activity seems to have disappeared in CBC
  2. Also, we are facing new challenges related to business role restriction in which FSV restriction does not behave similarly when FSVs are created through Manage Financial Statement Version app (FSV visible in restriction) or Manage FSV in Global Hierarchies (FSV missing in Restriction).

Any help on that would be much appreciated.

Best Regards,

Romain

entry.title: Re: Announcement: New release of content and central entry point for SAP BTP Solution Diagrams
entry.content: Is it possible to embed the used fonts inside the PPT template, or use fonts available both on Mac also?

entry.title: Difference between database view and table
entry.content:
Hello,

we are facing an S4Hana migration and in our Quality systems showed up a very strange issue, in the view NSDM_MIG_MSSL when I search for one material the values are different from table MSSL where the view takes the table from.

Why could be this happen since as far as I know the view is not a different table. 

I am missing something?

entry.title: Re: Error in Trigger
entry.content: I used "SBO_SP_TransactionNotification" before performing the trigger. But the table is a User Table of type "document" so it is difficult to find the objecType.

entry.title: Process Order With Different Resource Than In Recipe
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We have one material to which we have assigned X production version. This production version has recipe and BOM. The recipe has two resources say A & B. While creating the process order for this material as per standard process SAP should copy resource A & B into the order which are assigned to recipe. But every time on order creation only resource A got copy to order and resource B got automatically changed to C or D or any other resource. This is happening every time we create the order.

Can you please guide me what causing this error?

entry.title: Re: [SAP B1] License and Authorization Concern
entry.content: As far as I know the license valid for SAP to be taxed will be the one which has the user once the annual audit is done. So if at that moment this user has the license corresponding to a lower level or less expensive one this one will be the taken, the same if the user is inactive/blocked...

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hello Henry,

the Custom Code Migration app F3191 is only relevant, other apps are technically sub-apps of the Custom Code Migration app (Scoping, Analysis, Usage), therefore the are also listed in the Fiori Library, but they cannot be placed on the Fiori Launchpad, they don't have tiles.

KInd Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: User Assignment in SAC using SCIM API is failing for large number of records
entry.content:
Hi All, 
Hi @Matthew_Shaw 

Can you please let us know is there is any API other than SCIM which can be used for mass roles/teams assignmnets to users in SAC?

We need to assign more than 500 roles/teams to users in single API Call.

Currently we are using SCIM API to assign users/teams in SAC using custom API web application built on ABAP. The API runs fine for around 230 records but when it exceeds the number(~230) the assignment operation fails without any error. There are no logs at SAC side and neither we can track anything in web application. 

Regards,
Jagan

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

Let us know whenever we can support, and please feel free to take part in discussions in any open group or start a discussion of your own. Why not check out our location groups and Coffee Corner for starters?!

Nikhil Save

entry.title: Re: Conversion Exits and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP)

entry.title: Graph intro series, part 4: Hello Graph! Write your first Graph application
entry.content:
This is the first of the end-to-end implementation blog series for Graph that focuses on a developer writing an extension application that uses the single, unified API of Graph in SAP Integration Suite. Please refer to Part 1 of this series for an introduction to Graph and to the Information Map for an introduction to the entire series.

The initial developer parts in this series will lead you through building the rudimentary basics of a classical enterprise extension web app: a list-details-navigate application in the next part. This is what it will look like:

Here, in this part, our goal is to establish the basic hello world programming essentials. This implementation requires no prior knowledge – we will teach you everything you need to know to become a proficient Graph developer. To test that everything works, we will re-use the Graph server that was configured as an SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox, and that we used in Part 1.

Getting Started: Node.js and NPM

We will use node.js (v20.11.1) and npm (version 10.2.4) to build this application and to keep it extremely simple, we will use the express web framework. Not familiar with node.js and express? I recommend this article on the Mozilla Developer Network (MDN) website. This article also explains how to easily set up a Node development environment, whether you use Windows, MacOS, or Linux.

If you prefer to develop applications in the cloud directly from your browser, SAP offers a great cloud-based development experience alternative in the form of the SAP Build. Follow this link for more details on getting your instance. The rest of this blog assumes that you installed node.js and npm on your computer.

package.json

Select a folder (directory) where you want to develop your application. In this folder, create a small file called package.json, with the following content:

 

{
    "name": "hello-graph",
    "version": "0.0.0",
    "private": true,
    "type": "module",
    "script" : : {
        "start": "node src/hellograph.js"
    },
    "dependencies": {
        "express": "4.18.1",
        "node-fetch": "3.3.2",
        "universal-cookie": "7.1.0"
    }
}

 

Note: For the latest version of these libraries please refer to https://www.npmjs.com/

After saving the file, run the following command on your console:

npm install

If your node.js environment was properly set up, then this command will install a few standard library packages in a new sub-folder called node_modules.
Now, create another sub-folder, next to node_modules, called src. This is where we will develop our code.

graph.js

First, we will create a boilerplate file in the src folder, called graph.js. This file will serve as a foundational template that you'll reuse, with a few small changes, across all future Graph implementation blogs and your projects. This file will contain a class called Graph, which provides a nice wrapper for using Graph. At this time, we will show how to read data, using a get() function.

Copy and paste the code below into the file, then save it. This straightforward snippet utilizes the node-fetch package for simplicity.

 

import fetch from "node-fetch";
export default class Graph {
    async get(entity, params) {
        const url = `https://sandbox.api.sap.com/sapgraph/${entity}${params ? `?${params}` : ""}`;
        console.log(url) //for debugging 
        const options = {
            method: "get",
            headers:{
                "Accept": "application/json",
                "apiKey": "<your-api-key>" // TODO: Enter the actual key
            }
        };
        const response = await fetch(url, options);
        console.log(`${response.status} (${response.statusText})`) // for debugging
        return await response.json();
    }
}

 

Please note that we hard-coded the Graph server that uses the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox. This allows us to focus here on the data access API, without requiring you to configure a new Business Data Graph(BDG) and deal with all the complex aspects of security and authentication, which will be the subject of future blog series.
Since we are accessing the sandbox landscape data via SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you will need to insert your API Key (a short string) into the code above. Where do you get this key? Simply log in to https://api.sap.com/settings and click on Show API Key to see and save it.

hellograph.js

Now we are ready to write our first, simple server-side application that uses Graph. Paste the following into a new file, called hellograph.js, in the src folder:

 

import express from 'express';
import Graph from './graph.js';

const app = express();
const port = 3004;
const graph = new Graph();

app.get('/sap*', async (req, res) => {
    const response = await graph.get(req, `${req.url}`, "");
    res.send(`<pre><code>${JSON.stringify(response, null, 2)}</code></pre>`);
});

app.listen(port, () => { 
    console.log(`Explore Graph at http://localhost:${port}`) 
});

 

As previously mentioned, our code uses a popular node.js package called express. On line 8, we define the action taken when our server-side app is called from a browser. Here, we simply take the URL that we received (req.url) and pass it through to Graph. We then show the returned data from Graph as a raw result on the browser screen.

Our server-side app is ready. To run it, use your terminal console to enter:

npm run start

The console will show:

$ Explore Graph at http://localhost:3004 

Now the fun begins. The application expects a well-formed query URL to function properly. Open a browser window or tab, and enter the following query URL:

http://localhost:3004/sap.graph/SalesQuote?$top=2

The browser will invoke your application code, which will call Graph to fetch the data, and if all goes well, you will see the following output on your screen. 

Note: if you get an unformatted result, you can install a JSON formatter in your browser. 
Our Hello Graph app is already pretty useful; we can use it to explore the business data graph, by trying out different OData queries. Note: to stop your app from running, simply kill it (e.g. press CTRL-C on your Linux terminal or Windows console).
We will re-use this app as a skeleton in the next part of this series, where we will introduce authentication.

Shardul Sonar, Developer – Graph in SAP Integration Suite

Learn more about Graph in the SAP Community

entry.title: Set Initialize Value for Purchase Order in S4HANA
entry.content:
I would like to initialize values for custom fields in this BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PO_INITIALIZE.

But I can't call the custom fields in this BAdI. Is there any ways to call custom fields in this BAdI?

entry.title: Issue with System Copy for S/4HANA server
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are performing a system copy, so as to replicate Production DB on our training system. Our production landscape is on S/4HANA 2023, and we are creating a (new) training environment on S/4HANA 2021.

At last (when 94% execution is done) of SWPM steps, we are getting an error "DBIF_SETG_BUFFER_TOO_SHORT" and we are unable to figure out any solution for that. Please help in resolving this error. 

Regards,

Bhavya

entry.title: Re: Queue error for CIF article master data transfer
entry.content: Hello @s0m30n3 Could you please check if Supply chain units are maintained in /n/scmb/scumain

entry.title: Re: Cloud API Enablement - FAQ
entry.content:
Thank you Alexander for this detailed and very helpful summary. 

Is there an update regarding "when a wrapper in tier 2 itself is considered Clean Core", or which channel to check for upcoming update?

entry.title: Rejected PRs could be found in KOB2
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

In our system, we could find rejected PRs in KOB2 report under layout 2SAP, this could be misleading information for the colleagues, how could we make these rejected PRs disappear?

In our system the users are not allowed to set deletion flag by PRs, but we could set closed mark by quantities/dates, and I think it is also helpful when we could make the "rejected" displayed in KOB2 report.

Thank you very much for your help!

entry.title: FSCD Training Materials
entry.content: Do you have any idea where can I find training materials for SAP Collections and Disbursement module?

entry.title: Re: Service Consumption Model 2 for OData Client Proxy
entry.content:
Hello,

in the Service Entity Set Details we find code snippets for all basic operations. Would it be possible to also extend the examples with deep insert, deep update and read with expand. I found blog entries which describe it(deep insert, read with expand ) but it would be handy to have it also with the other snippets.

BR
Hendrik

entry.title: Re: New Changes Coming to SAP PartnerEdge Program in 2024
entry.content:
Hi @JerryRosa 

this is an exceptionally helpful blog post for early stage tech startups like ours https://www.atkrypto.io , early stage tech startups who are building Products which leverage existing Customer investments in SAP BTP and SAP Cloud and S/4HANA for the benefit of SAP Customers.

Our company https://www.atkrypto.io is on the journey towards becoming a SAP Partner Edge Build Partner, and I have a few questions regarding the SAP Partner Edge onboarding process  and blogging on the SCN.

Please allow me to introduce the subject and then the questions, where your thoughts and advice will be very much welcomed and appreciated.

atkrypto.io flagship product is the atkrypto,ioEnterprise Blockchain Platform, and the uniqueness of the product crosses a number of dimensions including, the only Enterprise Blockchain Software which can be installed from the Edge and Mobile and IoT Devices all the way across to the DataCenter enabling the same Enterprise Blockchain to run from the Edge to the DataCenter. 

We, the founders of atkrypto.io are SAP Experts, I have been a SAP Consultant for 25 years and actually I am a former SCN Moderator and Mentor Alumni, https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-blog-posts/andy-silvey-scn-moderator-spotlight/ba-p/13054438

We have designed the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform from the outset to benefit SAP Customers and a major part of our Go To Market is to launch the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP on the SAP Store as part of the SAP Partner Edge Build Program.

And atkrypto.io synergies to your blog are very clear, atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP is built to run on the SAP BTP Business Technology Platform Kyma Runtime and leverage SAP integration products, SAP Advanced Event Mesh, SAP Cloud Integration, SAP API Management and natively integrate with S/4HANA and other SAP Products and non-SAP Products. The goal of atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP is to make Blockchain easy for SAP Customers.

Our solution architecture designs for atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain for SAP leveraging SAP BTP and SAP Cloud Products and S/4HANA are numerous, here's an example:

Deployment Options leveraging SAP BTP and Cloud Products for atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP:

 

Questions towards the SAP Partner Program

1) Open Eco System Partner Blogging on SCN

Back in the day, when I was an SCN Moderator, there we quite strict guidelines regarding to what level SAP Partners can Blog on the SCN about their solutions.

Hence my question, atkrypto.io and the solution atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP is now a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner.

Are SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partners allowed to Blog on the SAP Community about their solutions and how their solutions benefit both SAP and SAP Customers ?

We would like to Blog on the SCN about atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP and how Distributed Ledger Technology can benefit SAP Customers and how SAP Customers can leverage their existing SAP investments to implement the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime and integrate to their other SAP Products including S/4HANA etc.

The SAP Store Guide to Gaining Visibility recommends the Partner to Blog about their solution, https://store.sap.com/dcp/en/partner-with-us/documentation/go-to-market-guide/gaining-visibility-and-traffic

And hence the question, as  a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner can atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP be blogged on the SAP Community ? 

2) SAP Partner Edge Build Program Due Diligence Requirement for Employees in the Country of Company Registration

atkrypto.io, like many early stage tech startup companies which are going to market, is a Delaware (US) registered company and is registered as atkrypto inc.

Like many early stage tech startup companies, atkrypto inc is registered in Delaware (US) and the Directors do not reside in Delaware.

atkrypto inc has followed the the SAP Partner Edge Build onboarding process with the vision to launch the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP on the SAP Partner Edge Store, and atkrypto inc has failed the Due Diligence with the reason that, "SAP Partner Edge Build Program demands that partner companies have at least one employee in the country of registration of the company". 

As described above, like many many early stage tech startups, atkrypto.io's registered entity atkrypto inc is registered in Delaware and the Directors reside in other jurisdictions and not in Delaware. 

When we consider that the Partner Edge Build Program, https://partneredge.sap.com/content/partnerregistration/en_us/decisiontree.html , has ambitions to enable "Whether you’re a customer, service provider, VAR or independent software vendor, SAP PartnerEdge enables you to harness your intellectual property and rapidly take it to market under a single, comprehensive partner program. Start with Open Ecosystem: Build, to get access to SAP Business Technology Platformand other key resources that help you develop on and integrate with SAP solutions. When you’re ready to take your innovations to market, your transition to SAP PartnerEdge: Build will allow you to commercialize your solutions, whether they are SAP platform-based or integrated to SAP solutions",

would it be possible to progressively loosen the belt on the Due Diligence requirement that a SAP Partner Edge Build Partner has to have an employee in the country of registration of the company, considering that a good percentage of innovative tech startups and potential candidates for Partner Edge Build could be early stage tech startups where the registered entity is in one jurisdiction and the Directors reside in another jurisdiction ?

Thank you again for a very useful Blog, which really helps startups like ours to see that our product positioning is in line with SAP's ambitions for the Partner Edge program.

If you or colleagues can help with thoughts on these questions it will be very useful.

Andy Silvey

CEO atkrypto inc

https://www.atkrypto.io

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP

SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner

 

Blockchain #DistributedLedgerTechnology SAP BTP, Kyma runtime SAP Global Batch Traceability SAP BTP Security #MultiPartyCollaboration Cloud Integration #startups #partnerEdge #SAPStore #Partnerships

entry.title: Re: How to work with CAP CDS > 7 ? .How do i access and m...
entry.content:
HI @Subramaniyam , as you can see theres already an accepted Solution to this Issue. And your Answer doesnt solve this since we were talking about Reading and not Modifying Filters.

Im also totally aware of how to modify Querys. But if we talk about this i Think, that your approach is actually less Readable and more complex than it has to be. Since parsing is handled automatically before query Excecution, you should be able to just push your own Filterin a more readble object literal or string format to the .where of said req.query object

Best Regards,
Michael

entry.title: Where do you find list of BAPIs and Remote Function Modules related to FI and CO?
entry.content: Is there such a list so that external applications can read and post to SAP?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - KPI is inactive error in BPMon
entry.content:
Hi Paula,

Thank you for responding.

Yes, we have all the current notes applied in the business suite systems. Also, the BPMon related jobs do not have any error in the logs. Application logs also do not have any error. 

I do not see that error occurring today. Do the KPIs get activated/deactivated automatically? Also, is there a way to see all the active & inactive KPIs for a particular system? [I don't want to drill-down on each end-to-end process and then see the KPIs for the respective sub-processes/process-steps. Instead looking for a central view showing the list system wise either on Cloud ALM side or managed system side. Configuration -> KPI Management -> does give me the list, but it doesn't help filter it system wise or export the list with details on which system the data collection for the KPI is active.]

entry.title: GR'ing at a different Price
entry.content:
Greetings, I had a question I was hoping the community could help with.

I have PO with a single line at a Qty of 5. The total GR value for that line is $500 ($100 x 5)

However physically this delivery was not 5 units of 1 Each, but it was actually a single box containing 5 bottles. So the PO Qty should have actually been 1.

Our warehouse then reversed the GR Qty of 5, the PO Qty was then altered to 1, and our warehouse then GR'd this 1 unit. However the GR value is now only $100, instead of the $500 we have been invoiced for.

Can anyone suggest any ideas on how I can fix this so the GR Qty is 1, and the GR Value if $500?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: 回复： The Sales Order Confirmation PDF Output lack the cust...
entry.content:
Hi Ramesh_vestrics,

1. You can edit the sales order ,and click this button . The sales order must be in the change status.

2. you can add output here.

3. when you add a new output , you can click the button display details.

4. in the details , you can enter the print queue:#&&DOWNLOAD_FORM_DATA_XML&&#

5. Leave output request, and you can click the button display document, and then you can download the XML now.

Please provide a solution.

But I do not know how to migrate the reports/schedules/users/groups/folders/permissions from the old server to new server by keeping everything intact. Would you please share me your thoughts on this scenario? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to connect with "Microsoft Power Apps" or "Micros...
entry.content:
Hi @Martin-Pankraz 

Thank you for your reply.
Recently, I started studying "Integration Suite" and "Microsoft Power Platform".
For that reason, I'm sorry that my understanding is insufficient.
Therefore, I would like to ask the following questions again.
① Push type scenario
(1)Click the button installed in Power Apps. (trigger)
(2) Power Automate will start and retrieve the preset "Dataverse" data.
(3) Obtain information using "Cloud integration (integration flow)" for which HTTP connection settings have been made in advance from the "HTTP connector" set in advance in Power Automate.
(*Are you not using "SAP Open Connectors" or "SAP API Management"?)

②Pull type scenario
(1) Actions on the “Integration Suite” side (event mesh, etc. (trigger))
(2) Connect to “Dataverse” using “Cloud Integration (Integration Flow)” (*Using “Open Connector”?)
I couldn't figure out how to connect to "Microsoft Power Automate" or "Dataverse" using "Open Connectors".

I would like to know more details if possible.
Best Regards.
Yuya

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite; Iterate over Value and write i...
entry.content:
@Ryan-Crosby Thanks for your help. I have done it now a little bit different but it is working like i want it to work.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM
entry.content: Hi Ajit, good day! our warehouse with MFS and automated. BIN depth is front and behind bin, for stock removal case outbound, warehouse task created by ADHU(Mass creation also) for the behind bin pallet, but already front bin have pallet and no warehouse task for front one. that is resource while trying to access pallet from behind bin for existing WT, error throws front bin blocked /occupied. Solution to be provided is at ADHU while creating WT need to validate if front bin occupied and no WT, then internal transfer this front pallet to same SKU other destination bin. after that behind bin pallet WT will continue to removal., advance thanks for the valuable suggestion.

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/expand-option?locale=en-US&version=Cloud 
https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/odata-request-deep-create?locale=en-US&version=Cloud

Best regards
Bernhard

entry.title: Re: User Assignment in SAC using SCIM API is failing for...
entry.content:
Jagan

Firstly, thank you so much for posting here. It will help organise our discussion better than comments to a blog post.

I would be amazed if the problem was with the SCIM API, it's almost certain the issue is with your hosting ABAP application. 

To update team membership, you must use the /Groups/TEAMID endpoint. There are different workflows you could use to add users, for example, you could add a user at a time, or you could add many users. I discuss this in a great deal in my best practices article 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/boc/3354605866.html#SAPAnalyticsCloudUserandTeamProvisioningSCIMAPIBestPracticeandAPIPerformance-Teamupdates

My article suggests the most optimised means to update a team and my sample script uses all the best practices. For example, I share that adding 500 users to an empty team could take 13 seconds, instead of 13 minutes!  So it will be interesting to see if the ABAP program has been used as its basis. Did you use my sample scripts and did it adopt the best practice? 

The scim API was also updated recently and this allows for a PATCH request this is handy if you need to make the logic very simple in your application since only a single request is needed. There would be no need to GET and then PUT, it can all be done in a single PATCH request. You might find my blog handy to explain some other improvements that have come with the scim API update. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-analytics-cloud-scim-api-version-1-and-2-high-level-comparison/ba-p/13578698

Do feel free to share more details. My SAP colleagues will also be happy to forward an email to me, so we can discuss this directly if you think that would help.

All the best

Matthew

entry.title: Re: Planning strategy for header material VC and SFG MTS
entry.content: Hi, You can use this strategy for KMAT FG also without variants. When you maintain the PIR for FG without usage probabilities for characteristics, FG BOM will explode only with the underlying SFG in case if it is the only component without object dependencies. (PIR>Schedule lines >configuration supporting point).

entry.title: Instance Sync & Configure People Profile.
entry.content:
Hello

I am going to share one of the issues faced while moving the Configure People profile changes from one instance to another. We have selected the EmployeeProfilePageConfig in MDF Object definitions and moved the same. However, on checking on target system we could find that the changes were not transported, and we were getting an error as in note 3409204.However as per note we would have to make some changes to the object by going in to Manage Data-->EmployeeProfilePageConfig .However on checking the same there were no results. As a solution we did move the data using MDF Data-->EmployeeProfilePageConfig then we could see the object in Manage data and also the configuration in "Configure People Profile" as expected.

Hence be sure to move the data as well for changes to appear in Configure People Profile Page.

entry.title: Re: TU transaction in SAP EWM S4/HANA

entry.title: Re: Re-assign a WI to a different WP?
entry.content:
Hello Marianne, 

Please see point 3 of the following KBA: 

2894753 - Focused Build: Performing advanced functionalities for Work Items

It should be possible to reassign Work Items in status "Successfully Tested", status "Handover to release" is the point of no return.

It shouldn't be necessary to decouple the transport requests as long as the Work Package you are swapping to is assigned to a release using the same landscape.

Please note there needs to be at least one Work Item assigned to the Work Package after the reassignment, so a dummy Work Item may be needed to facilitate the reassignment. 

I hope this information is helpful.

Regards,
Kerrie
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: "Statement is not accessible." - Error
entry.content: check if the query is inside START-OF-SELECTION ... END-OF-SELECTION, you could check if the previous query has ended with dot(.)

entry.title: Re: Picking
entry.content:
Hello Bilkiss

Already confirmed ? do you mean already picked ? in that case you do cancel pick , else please explain in detail 

BR Shazeb

entry.title: Supporting crypto agility with the BTP Trust Store
entry.content:
You have all heard that change is the new normal. This is true in many ways, including for IT security. Whether it is the cryptographic algorithm, the key length, or an individual key, we must always be ready to change to something “better”, namely more secure. This might not even be due to an attack, but rather be part of continuous efforts to stay ahead of all potential threats.

When it comes to protocols like Transport Layer Security (TLS), the level of security also depends on the root certificates that are the anchor of trust for the TLS-protected communication. In the past, the validity of root certificates could be decades, so a change would be a once in a lifetime thing. Today however, even though the root certificates are not really short-lived, they will still have to be replaced now and then.

Changing the trust anchor of communication in a large landscape requires some preparation, to ensure that everybody can communicate securely and without disruption. If you miss to update the trust configuration across all involved parties, then the change of a root certificate may cause an outage for all scenarios that rely on the trust.

When SAP changes the root certificates that are the trust anchors of BTP, we ensure that all scenarios inside the platform are ready, and the communication is not affected. However, you may also have clients or 3rd party applications that integrate with BTP. For these, the trust is not managed by SAP and so you need to take matters into your own hands.

With the new BTP Trust Store, we want to help you avoid outages by providing information about changes in the trust anchors of SAP BTP early. This gives you the time to roll out new root certificates before they become mandatory.

You will find the required root certificates in several well-known formats in the BTP Trust Store at https://github.com/sap-software/btp-trust-store

The trust store includes 2 sets of root certificates:

The “Required” root certificates are mandatory for all clients or services that communicate via TLS with SAP BTP-based services. Ensure that your clients or services trust these root certificates, and that changes to the list of root certificates are reflected early on your side, such as once per quarter. This will ensure that you can continue to communicate with BTP even if SAP changes root certificates over time.

The “Optional” set of root certificates represents the SAP Global Trust List provided by SAP Global Security, as documented in SAP Note 2801396.

entry.title: Are there any best practice guidelines for client-specific Customizing in EML for S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Are there any best practice guidelines, recommendations, or restrictions suggested by SAP for client-specific Customizing or Configuration in the Enterprise Management Layer (EML) for SAP S/4HANA greenfield implementation? Or can the Customizing be done similarly to any other S/4HANA On-Premise implementations?

RISE with SAP SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud SAP S/4HANA SAP Activate 

@Petra_Ohlinger

entry.title: Re: Running Hana Express on M1 Macs
entry.content:
Works great, thank you.
However, to execute more demanding scripts I had to check Force Multicore box in System settings and manually set number of cores otherwise Hana became unresponsive. 
"Force multicore must be selected when attempting to emulate multiple cores of a strong memory system on a weak memory system (strong-on-weak). A typical example of strong-on-weak is attempting to emulate x86_64/i386 on an ARM host. In these cases, QEMU does not guarantee the correctness of the emulation. You must also manually specify the number of cores when forcing multicore."

entry.title: Re: User Assignment in SAC using SCIM API is failing for...
entry.content: Thank you, but I think the question is about SCIM rather than SAML. Both can be used for user to team assignment, though the SAML option is very popular, it means the assignment of the team only occurs at the time of the user login and not before. SCIM resolves this problem and a few others. Your blog is handy though for those that want to understand the SAML option for team assignment, so thanks for sharing that. All the best, Matthew

entry.title: Re: How Can I Find Detailed Instructions on Setting up Pr...
entry.content: Noel, it will take me a few minutes to try working through the instructions and comments you provided. I did not want to wait until then to tell you, your response is astonishingly excellent. You seem to have covered all the details needed, with exceptional awareness and focus on the perspective I happen to have. This response demonstrates a special gift for writing support information. I will let you know how it goes when I try applying what you have written.

entry.title: Re: How change the Iview property -> SAP Gui Type in Mass...
entry.content:
Hi,

There is no way to change the iView property in mass, you have to go one by one in eacj iView and do the changes.

Regards,

Rohit Gera

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget & Widget Add-Ons Samples Preview
entry.content:
Background 

Now that Custom Widget is available with the Unified Story Experience since QRC/Q2 - 2023, and Widget Add-Ons since QRC/Q4 - 2023, we received a large amount of requests coming from SAC users searching for Samples in order to extend the Built-in set of visualisations in SAC.

In this article, I will highlight the Ready-To-Use Custom Widget Samples available in the SAP Public GitHub repository which you can download, edit and consume within SAP Analytics Cloud. You can use these Samples as a starting point to learn how to develop Custom Widget.

In addition I will highlight a Widget Add-on sample which you can use in order to extend the visual elements of the Built-in charts such a Bar chart, and with that you will have the possibility to modify the Tooltip or the Plot area by overwriting existing visual elements content or style.

Note: this article will be always updated every time we develop new Custom Widget Sample. Feel free to let us know your most requested visualisation that you wish to see as Custom Widget in the future in the comment section. Partners and Customers who wants to include their Custom Widget or Widget Add-on samples in this article, they can contact me so I can refer to their blog posts, and with that we can gather more type of visualisation and make it visible for everyone.

Custom Widget Samples: Preview

The Custom Widget Samples that I will preview in this article, are ready to be used within your Story. You can download the resource files and then upload it into your SAC tenant.

You can edit the Custom Widgets by modifying the source code from the resource files if it's required or even extend the Custom Widget by adding new web component files like: Styling.js, in case you wish adding some new settings to the sample. So let's get started with the first preview of our Samples.

Sankey Chart

You can download the Sankey Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set measure, set an hierarchical dimension and edit the default colors of the different depths from the Styling panel.

You can refer to this sample in case you want to understand how to add new settings into the Styling panel by creating the Styling.js file.

Gauge Chart

 

You can download the Gauge Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set your measure and from the styling panel set your threshold.

Custom Pie Chart

You can download the Custom Pie Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set your measure and your dimension. 

Bar Gradient Chart

You can download the Bar Gradient Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set your measure and your dimension. 

Funnel Chart

You can download the Funnel Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set your measure and your dimension. You can export the Funnel chart as image from the top right icons, or also edit the data values.

Nested Pie Chart

You can download the Nested Pie Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set your measure and your dimension.

Datepicker

You can download the Datepicker from here. This Custom Widget provides a sophisticated input field for date values and date ranges. The date formatting is customizable and a dark mode is provided.

 Line Chart

You can download the Line Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set your measure and your dimension.

Half Doughnut Chart

You can download the Half Doughnut Chart from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add it to your Unified Story Experience (Optimized mode), from the builder panel, select your data model, set your measure and your dimension.

File Upload Widget

The file upload widget extends upon the Data Import API to allow users to upload their FactData CSV or Excel datasets to public and private versions. The widget serves as an intermediary tool, parsing the file and sending the data contained within it to the Data Import API. You can refer to this blog to know more about this Custom Widget.

Widget Add-on Samples: Preview

The Widget Add-on Samples are also ready to be used within your Story. You can download the resource files and upload it into your SAC tenant, then from the builder panel you can add the widget add-on to the supported charts to modify the tooltip or the plot area. Learn more how to enable the widget add-on within your story here.

Sample 1

You can download this sample from here, upload it into your SAC tenant, add your supported chart (such Bar chart) into the canvas, enable the widget add-on from the builder panel and add your plot area and tooltip. you can edit either the tooltip or the plot area in the design time, but modify the settings from the builder panel, for example you can change the default icons with your own ones.

More blogs to check out 👇

  • How to use Linked Analysis with Custom Widget
  • Custom Widget Hands-on Guide 2023
  • Announcing the new SAP Analytics Cloud feature: Widget Add-on
  • Hosting and Uploading Custom Widgets Resource Files into SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Custom Widget Samples GitHub Repository

entry.title: Re: Error in Trigger
entry.content: @ANKIT_CHAUHAN, can you help? How can we determine the objectType of a user defined table?

entry.title: Re: How Can I Find Detailed Instructions on Setting up Pr...
entry.content: I have no idea what I did to produce the pink box of extraneous letters.

entry.title: Re: SAP Portal upgrade from current version SAP NW 7.50,...
entry.content:
Hi Prasad,

Below will be the Pre and Post Checks of SP Upgrade:

  • Test all the System Alias.
  • Test all the destination in nwa.
  • Check all the custom Portal applicaiton and keep the back up of the applicaiton.
  • Test the JCO's Destination if any.
  • Test and verify the SAP Backend certificate.

These are basic activities which you have to perofrom as Pre and Post Upgrade. As it is SP upgrade so there wont be much imact on the system.

 

Regards,

Rohit Gera

entry.title: Re: Looking Back at the Success of SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM
entry.content:
Good day! As per my understanding, it looks like universal features of ASRS... still have questions like "What is ADHU and why did you create in mass and how?" ...etc And hard to comment "WHY" you did this, Sorry. Furthermore  Please have a look at below link 

https://wmexperts.online/2022/04/30/mfs-rearrange-multi-depth-bins/

Prima facie need custom code with solution provided by Hendrik with system set up. Telegrams would be back and forth for such activities with PLC as per your system design. Please read carefully.

Best,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: IBP - Editable key figure only editable on lowest (ba...
entry.content: @vincentverheyen, actually I learnt this from an issue. One of our users had authorization issue, he didn't have access to one of the lowest level planning objects when changing data on a higher level. So I thought this error-driven behaviour could help with your requirement. And yes, this would affect other key figures as well.

entry.title: EWM x DM integration - PSA stock not read
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm struggling with the DM x EWM integration. However it seems that my stock is staged in DM but I can't see the stock in the "manage stock floor" application. Any help / idea's? 

My assumption is that it would work out of the box.

(EDI via SOAMANAGER is configured correctly.)

entry.title: Event sender stopped, it gives SAP B1iP detected errorInternal Reason:Name expected: 0x7d (:m
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP Event sender stopped running. Before some days it was working. When start Event sender setup wizard. It gives following Error. 

Error gives by Event sender from B1if:SAP B1iP detected an error:Emitted HTTP-Code:500 - Internal Server Error

Internal Reason:

Name expected: 0x7d (:main:, row:2, col:180)

Recommendation:
Check for the correctness of your activity or environment or ask your system-administrator for further help.

Full Internal Error Message:

com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.XcelleratorException: XCE001 Nested exception:
com.sap.b1i.bizprocessor.BizProcException: BPE001 Nested exception: com.sap.b1i.utilities.UtilException: UTE001 Nested exception: com.sap.engine.lib.xml.parser.ParserException: Name expected: 0x7d(:main:, row:2, col:180)

 

Log-ID:

LID24022810582451406050C0A8100A7F9B

Thanking you in advance,

entry.title: Re: [SAP B1] License and Authorization Concern
entry.content:
Hi,

As far as I know, the authorization system and the license system are two separate layers that determine what a user can see and do.

Theoretically, when you set authorizations for a user, with a professional license assigned, these settings will remain when you change the license type to a limited one. The only effect should be that certain functionality will be blocked all of a sudden. When you change the license type to professional later on, all will look the same, but the previously blocked functionality will now be accesible again.

Fortunately it is relatively easy to create a demo company. Just create a new empty database, and restore a backup of your production database on top of it (with overwrite). Once you have this test database, you can simply test it.

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Re: How to Choose the Control Account When Creating Journ...
entry.content: cc

entry.title: Date Dimension in SAC
entry.content:
Hi 

I have story filter FY Period hierarchy selection and Date dimension also maintain Fiscal year period hierarchy as default hierarchy. My Date dimension data as below (Calendar year Month is Key value).

Date Dimension DataFiscal Year Period Hierarchy Setting

I am calculating simple multiplication of two KFs and saving to another KF through advance formula.  I have created FYMonth_Input parameter with settings (Hierarchy: fiscal Year period, Level: Any). 

MEMBERSET [d/CompCode] = (%CCODE_Input%)
MEMBERSET [d/SalesGroup] = (%SGroup_Input%)
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = (BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/FYP], %FYMonth_Input%))

DATA([d/Measures] = "Sales_Output") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Sales_Input", [d/CompCode] = %CCODE_Input%, [d/SalesGroup] = %SGroup_Input%) * RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Sales_Forecast", [d/CompCode] = %CCODE_Input%, [d/SalesGroup] = %SGroup_Input%)

The advance formula working and providing multiplication of two KFs. But I would like to add Date parameter as well in RESULTLOOKUP, but it is not allowing me to add Date parameter.

The story filter will pass the Date parameter value, hence I would like to include Date parameter as well in RESULTLOOKUP to get the KF values for same fiscal year period.

I don't know how it works, The RESULTLOOKUP will consider the MEMBERSET [d/Date] parameter input or not. If not consider KF values will aggregate other months which is not correct. 

And If I change FY Period Parameter settings  level to Leaf (Hierarchy: fiscal Year period, Level: Leaf), then it allowing to add Date parameter in RESULTLOOKUP, This time MEMBERSET [d/Date] is just reading from parameter not from hierarchy structure (not like above code).

MEMBERSET [d/CompCode] = (%CCODE_Input%)
MEMBERSET [d/SalesGroup] = (%SGroup_Input%)
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = (%FYMonth_Input%)
DATA([d/Measures] = "Sales_Output") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Sales_Input", [d/CompCode] = %CCODE_Input%, [d/SalesGroup] = %SGroup_Input%, [d/Date] = (%FYMonth_Input%)) * RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Sales_Forecast", [d/CompCode] = %CCODE_Input%, [d/SalesGroup] = %SGroup_Input%, [d/Date] = (%FYMonth_Input%))

There is no errors in code but it is not providing any result.

Please explain me, why this code is not working even it allowed Date parameter in RESULTLOOKUP.

And the first code is working without allowing Date parameter in RESULTLOOKUP but my worry is may aggregate the KF values to other months.

Regards,

Vasu

entry.title: Re: API operations for Custom Business Objects in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Varun,

Thanks for the detailed blog, is it possible to call the CBO inbound service url in the badi implementation in custom logic app to post an entry in CBO? could you please share how to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano,

I hope this email finds you well. My name is Dang Hai NGUYEN, and I am currently working as a .NET Developer at Lotte Data Communication in Vietnam. I am writing to express my interest in obtaining a free voucher for the SAP CER01 certification exam.

As a dedicated professional in the SAP ecosystem, I believe that obtaining this certification would significantly enhance my skills and contribute to my career growth. However, due to financial constraints, I find the exam fee to be challenging.

I have thoroughly prepared for the CER01 exam and am eager to demonstrate my competence in SAP technologies. I kindly request your assistance in providing me with a free voucher for this certification.

If there are any additional steps or requirements, please let me know, and I will promptly comply.

Thank you for considering my request. I appreciate your support in advancing my professional journey. I look forward to your positive response.

Thanks and Best regards,

Dang Hai NGUYEN
